If the surroundings feel upscale and performance-driven without neon chaos, you are in the right corridor. Take a team photo that proves you found the arts-and-music checkpoint.

kickoff_challenge: Find something blue in the lobby or a street sign outside and photograph it.
participant_task_pre_solve: Take a team photo featuring the most recognizable landmark element in the area.
host_verification_instruction: Team photo with St. Louis Cathedral visible.
clue_text: Find the public plaza where painters line the perimeter and portrait artists call out to passersby. Iron fencing frames open space while cathedral towers dominate the skyline behind it. A mounted historical figure stands watch over the center and appears in countless travel photos. This is one of the strongest visual crossroads of history, art, and tourism in the Quarter. If street performers, open-air artwork, and church spires all appear together, you are close. Take a team photo featuring the most recognizable landmark element in the area.

kickoff_challenge: Find something blue in the lobby or a street sign outside and photograph it.
participant_task_pre_solve: Take a photo proving your team completed the signature treat challenge at this stop.
host_verification_instruction: Photo of powdered sugar mustache.
clue_text: Follow the aroma of chicory coffee and sweet fried dough dusted in white sugar. Tables turn quickly here as people compare powdered-sugar smiles and napkin disasters. The stop sits near major Quarter footpaths and is almost always buzzing from morning through night. You are searching for a long-running local ritual that visitors treat like a required pilgrimage. Look for lines, green-and-white visual cues, and people balancing paper bags with warm pastries. Take a photo proving your team completed the signature treat challenge at this stop.

kickoff_challenge: Find something blue in the lobby or a street sign outside and photograph it.
participant_task_pre_solve: Take a group photo proving you found the market checkpoint.
host_verification_instruction: Group photo near the French Market sign.
clue_text: Move toward the long commercial corridor where open-air stalls and vendor rows stretch block after block. This area blends shopping, snacks, souvenirs, and constant pedestrian flow. It feels historic but active, with changing displays and a market rhythm throughout the day. You should see repeated booth-style setups instead of a single storefront destination. If the scene looks like a continuous bazaar near the Quarter edge, you are in the correct zone. Take a group photo proving you found the market checkpoint.

kickoff_challenge: Find something blue in the lobby or a street sign outside and photograph it.
participant_task_pre_solve: Record a 10-second celebration video at the final destination.
host_verification_instruction: Cajun dance video at Mulate's.
clue_text: Your finish is beyond the French Quarter, where the streets widen and old warehouse blocks replace the tight tourist maze. This last stop is known as the original Cajun restaurant, a high-energy place where Louisiana flavor, live music, and dancing come together under one roof. Look near the Convention Center for a destination that feels more like a Cajun party than a quiet dinner. If you are still in balcony-lined Quarter streets, keep going. If you are along the water or at the Outlets, you have gone too far. Record a 10-second celebration video at the final destination.
